learning_outcomes.description: Appraise the main structural components of eukaryotic cells, including the nucleus and cellular organelles, and evaluate their individual contribution to cellular function.

Identify, understand and relate the molecular components of the cell nucleus and organelles to their function with a special focus on cell division and cell differentiation in a variety of cell types.
Appreciate and connect molecular interactions within and between eukaryotic cells and to the non-cellular environment.
Explain how proteins are processed and transported within the cell after synthesis in order to perform their function.
Investigate experimental approaches that are used in molecular cell biology and identify the most appropriate ones to apply these to develop a strategy to address a current problem in the field.
To comprehend the molecular mechanisms of disease and how this understanding contributes to the development of new tools for diagnosis and treatment.

assessments.task_description:
The exam will cover modules 1-4 (The Nucleus, Cell Division, Cell Organelles and Cellular Homeostasis). Short answer questions on the content covered in the lectures. The number of questions and point value of questions from each staff member will reflect the number of lectures given by that staff member. Additional information will be provided on Blackboard and in class.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.assessment_title: Practical Assessment - Methods in Molecular Cell Biology Quizzes
assessments.due_date:
Quiz 1 22/09/2026 - 23/09/2026

Quiz 2 20/10/2026 - 21/10/2026

The quiz will be completed in your timetabled practical class.
assessments.task_description:
A combination of multiple choice and short answer questions on the learning objectives covered in each practical will be given in the second week of each practical exercise.

Each quiz will be worth 7.5% of the total assessment for the course.

This assessment item includes practical attendance completion and practical quiz assessments conducted during selected timetabled practical sessions. Please read the following carefully: 

If you are unable to attend your timetabled practical session due to circumstances beyond your control (e.g., illness), you must submit a practical absence request immediately, accompanied by acceptable supporting documentation that covers the date of the missed practical.

A list of acceptable reasons and required supporting information is available at: https://my.uq.edu.au/information-and-services/manage-my-program/exams-and-assessment/applying-assessment-extension?p=1#1

Requests for practical absences can be submitted via the course Blackboard site under the Assessment tab > Learning Resources > Practical Absence. Your request must be submitted prior to or on the day of the missed practical. It is your responsibility to submit an application and provide the correct documentation by the due date and time. Requests without appropriate supporting documentation may not be approved.

 If your absence occurs during a non-quiz practical week, you are only required to submit a practical absence request.

 If your absence occurs during a practical session in which a quiz is scheduled, you must:

  • Submit a practical absence request for the missed practical session; and
  • Submit an extension request for the practical quiz assessment component.

If the reason for your absence is not approved, you will be considered absent from that practical session and any applicable attendance or assessment requirements may apply.

If you are unable to complete a practical quiz because you were unable to attend the timetabled quiz session, follow the instructions listed under Deferral or Extension and submit an extension request with appropriate supporting documentation.

Students with an approved extension for one or both quizzes will be required to sit the quiz at a time and date as set by the Course Coordinator in week 13.

Please note that submitting a practical absence request does not automatically provide an extension for a quiz. If you miss a quiz held during a practical session, you must submit both a practical absence request and an extension request.

Do not submit a practical absence request in place of an extension application for the quiz assessment component. Follow the appropriate extension procedure outlined above.

If you require further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact the School - enquiries@scmb.uq.edu.au

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission will occur at the completion of your practical class.

assessments.task_description:
Students will work collaboratively to produce a PowerPoint presentation to pitch a research grant to investigate a novel cancer treatment. More information will be provided in the grant preparation workshops. The timetable for your group's presentation will be announced in the week before the first scheduled presentation. Each group will give their presentation to the whole class during one of the timetabled lecture periods.

Students will present a short PowerPoint presentation. The timetable for your group's presentation will be announced in the week before the first scheduled presentation. Each group will give their presentation to the whole class during one of the timetabled lecture periods. The assessment will also include asking questions to other groups about their research approaches following their presentations.

This assessment task is not eligible for a re-mark. In accordance with University policy, re-marks are not available the assessment has been marked by two or more examiners and post-marking moderation processes have been applied.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

The exam will cover modules 5-8 (Cellular Stress, Cell Differentiation, Cell Migration and Protein Trafficking). Short answer questions on the content covered in the second half of the course. It does not cover the content in the first half of the course that was covered in the In Semester Exam. The number of questions and point value of questions from each staff member will reflect the number of lectures given by that staff member. Additional information will be provided on Blackboard and in class.
